req.title: Hr-Admin views training-course
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a training-course through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for HR Admin Training Course View functionality.
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a training-course
through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record
must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from http import HTTPStatus


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """A fully authenticated HR-admin user."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "username": "hr_admin_01",
        "email": "hr.admin@example.com",
        "roles": ["hr-admin"],
        "is_active": True,
        "is_authenticated": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """An authenticated user without the hr-admin role."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "username": "regular_employee",
        "email": "employee@example.com",
        "roles": ["employee"],
        "is_active": True,
        "is_authenticated": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """An unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    return {
        "id": None,
        "username": None,
        "roles": [],
        "is_active": False,
        "is_authenticated": False,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def valid_training_course():
    """A complete, valid training course record."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "code": "TC-2024-001",
        "title": "Health & Safety Induction",
        "description": "Mandatory health and safety induction for all new starters.",
        "duration_minutes": 90,
        "category": "Compliance",
        "status": "ACTIVE",
        "created_at": datetime(2024, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "updated_at": datetime(2024, 3, 10, 14, 30, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "created_by": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "version": 3,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def training_course_missing_mandatory_fields():
    """
    A training course record that is present in the DB but has missing
    mandatory fields (simulates data-integrity violation).
    """
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        # 'code' is missing
        "title": None,          # mandatory but null
        "description": "Some description",
        "duration_minutes": None,  # mandatory but null
        "category": "Compliance",
        "status": "DRAFT",
        "created_at": None,     # mandatory but null
        "updated_at": None,
        "created_by": None,
        "version": 1,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    """Mock for the audit-log service."""
    with patch("app.services.audit_service.write_audit_record") as mock_audit:
        yield mock_audit


@pytest.fixture
def mock_training_course_repository():
    """Mock for the training course data repository."""
    with patch("app.repositories.training_course_repository.get_by_id") as mock_repo:
        yield mock_repo


@pytest.fixture
def api_client(hr_admin_user):
    """
    A test client pre-configured with hr-admin authentication headers.
    Replace with your actual framework's test client (Flask, FastAPI, Django…).
    """
    from app import create_app  # adjust import to your project structure

    app = create_app(testing=True)
    client = app.test_client()

    # Attach a valid JWT / session token for hr_admin_user
    token = _generate_test_token(hr_admin_user)
    client.environ_base["HTTP_AUTHORIZATION"] = f"Bearer {token}"
    return client


# Helper – replace with your project's token utility
def _generate_test_token(user: dict) -> str:
    """Generate a signed test JWT for the given user fixture."""
    import jwt as pyjwt  # PyJWT

    secret = "test-secret-key"
    payload = {
        "sub": user["id"],
        "username": user["username"],
        "roles": user["roles"],
        "exp": 9999999999,  # far-future expiry for tests
    }
    return pyjwt.encode(payload, secret, algorithm="HS256")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test class
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestViewTrainingCourse:
    """Tests covering GET /api/v1/training-courses/{id}"""

    BASE_URL = "/api/v1/training-courses"

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 1. Authentication & authorisation
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
        self, api_client, valid_training_course
    ):
        """An unauthenticated request must be rejected with 401 Unauthorized."""
        # Remove auth header
        api_client.environ_base.pop("HTTP_AUTHORIZATION", None)

        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_non_hr_admin_role_returns_403(
        self, api_client, non_hr_admin_user, valid_training_course
    ):
        """A user without hr-admin role must receive 403 Forbidden."""
        token = _generate_test_token(non_hr_admin_user)
        api_client.environ_base["HTTP_AUTHORIZATION"] = f"Bearer {token}"

        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.FORBIDDEN

    def test_hr_admin_is_authorised_to_view_course(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """An hr-admin must receive 200 OK when viewing an existing course."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.OK

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 2. Successful retrieval – response body
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_response_contains_all_mandatory_fields(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Response body must include every mandatory field with non-null values."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")
        data = response.get_json()

        mandatory_fields = ["id", "code", "title", "duration_minutes",
                            "category", "status", "created_at"]

        for field in mandatory_fields:
            assert field in data, f"Mandatory field '{field}' is missing from response"
            assert data[field] is not None, (
                f"Mandatory field '{field}' must not be null"
            )

    def test_response_id_matches_requested_course(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """The returned course id must match the id in the request URL."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course
        course_id = valid_training_course["id"]

        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{course_id}")
        data = response.get_json()

        assert data["id"] == course_id

    def test_response_fields_have_correct_types(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Mandatory fields must have the correct data types."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")
        data = response.get_json()

        assert isinstance(data["id"], str)
        assert isinstance(data["code"], str)
        assert isinstance(data["title"], str)
        assert isinstance(data["duration_minutes"], int)
        assert isinstance(data["category"], str)
        assert isinstance(data["status"], str)
        assert isinstance(data["created_at"], str)  # ISO-8601 string

    def test_response_content_type_is_json(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Response Content-Type must be application/json."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        assert "application/json" in response.content_type

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 3. Mandatory field validation
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_course_with_null_mandatory_fields_returns_422(
        self,
        api_client,
        training_course_missing_mandatory_fields,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """
        If a stored course has null mandatory fields the API must return
        422 Unprocessable Entity and must NOT write an audit record.
        """
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = (
            training_course_missing_mandatory_fields
        )

        response = api_client.get(
            f"{self.BASE_URL}/{training_course_missing_mandatory_fields['id']}"
        )

        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY
        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    def test_validation_error_response_identifies_missing_fields(
        self,
        api_client,
        training_course_missing_mandatory_fields,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """
        The 422 response body must list each field that failed validation.
        """
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = (
            training_course_missing_mandatory_fields
        )

        response = api_client.get(
            f"{self.BASE_URL}/{training_course_missing_mandatory_fields['id']}"
        )
        data = response.get_json()

        assert "errors" in data or "detail" in data, (
            "Response must contain an 'errors' or 'detail' key describing validation failures"
        )
        # At least the known null mandatory fields should be mentioned
        error_text = str(data).lower()
        assert "title" in error_text or "code" in error_text or "duration_minutes" in error_text

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 4. Not-found scenarios
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_nonexistent_course_returns_404(
        self,
        api_client,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Requesting a course that does not exist must return 404 Not Found."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = None
        non_existent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{non_existent_id}")

        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.NOT_FOUND

    def test_404_does_not_write_audit_record(
        self,
        api_client,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when the course is not found."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = None

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{uuid.uuid4()}")

        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    def test_invalid_uuid_format_returns_400(
        self,
        api_client,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """A malformed (non-UUID) course id must return 400 Bad Request."""
        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/not-a-valid-uuid!!!")

        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 5. Audit record – written on success
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_audit_record_written_on_successful_view(
        self,
        api_client,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """
        A single audit record must be written when an hr-admin successfully
        views a training course.
        """
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        mock_audit_service.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_action(
        self,
        api_client,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record action must be 'VIEW_TRAINING_COURSE'."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        audit_payload = kwargs.get("record") or mock_audit_service.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["action"] == "VIEW_TRAINING_COURSE"

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_id(
        self,
        api_client,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record must record the id of the hr-admin who performed the action."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        audit_payload = kwargs.get("record") or mock_audit_service.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["actor_id"] == hr_admin_user["id"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_resource_id(
        self,
        api_client,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record must reference the id of the viewed training course."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course
        course_id = valid_training_course["id"]

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{course_id}")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        audit_payload = kwargs.get("record") or mock_audit_service.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["resource_id"] == course_id

    def test_audit_record_contains_resource_type(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record must specify resource_type as 'TRAINING_COURSE'."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        audit_payload = kwargs.get("record") or mock_audit_service.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["resource_type"] == "TRAINING_COURSE"

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record must include a non-null timestamp."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        audit_payload = kwargs.get("record") or mock_audit_service.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload.get("timestamp") is not None

    def test_audit_record_contains_outcome_success(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record outcome must be 'SUCCESS' on a successful view."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        audit_payload = kwargs.get("record") or mock_audit_service.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["outcome"] == "SUCCESS"

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_failed_auth(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when authentication fails."""
        api_client.environ_base.pop("HTTP_AUTHORIZATION", None)

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_authorisation_failure(
        self,
        api_client,
        non_hr_admin_user,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when the caller lacks hr-admin role."""
        token = _generate_test_token(non_hr_admin_user)
        api_client.environ_base["HTTP_AUTHORIZATION"] = f"Bearer {token}"
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course

        api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 6. Audit-service failure does not swallow the response
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_audit_service_failure_returns_500(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """
        If the audit service raises an exception the API must return 500
        so the caller knows the operation could not be safely recorded.
        """
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course
        mock_audit_service.side_effect = RuntimeError("Audit store unavailable")

        response = api_client.get(f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}")

        assert response.status_code == HTTPStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 7. Idempotency – multiple views each produce an audit record
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_each_successful_view_produces_one_audit_record(
        self,
        api_client,
        valid_training_course,
        mock_training_course_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Each GET request by an hr-admin must produce exactly one audit record."""
        mock_training_course_repository.return_value = valid_training_course
        course_url = f"{self.BASE_URL}/{valid_training_course['id']}"

        api_client.get(course_url)
        api_client.get(course_url)
        api_client.get(course_url)

        assert mock_audit_service.call_count == 3
```

---

### Key design decisions

| # | Decision | Rationale |
|---|----------|-----------|
| 1 | **`mock_audit_service` & `mock_training_course_repository`** are session-scoped patch fixtures | Isolates every test from real I/O; failures are fast and deterministic |
| 2 | **Separate classes per concern** kept to one class here | The requirement is a single behaviour; sub-grouping by comment section keeps readability without over-engineering |
| 3 | **Audit record fields asserted individually** (`action`, `actor_id`, `resource_id`, `resource_type`, `timestamp`, `outcome`) | Requirement says "an audit record must be written" – granular assertions prove the record is correct, not just that the function was called |
| 4 | **`assert_not_called()`** on negative paths (401, 403, 404, 422) | Proves the audit service is only invoked on a true success |
| 5 | **Audit-service failure → 500** | Prevents silent loss of audit trail; the caller must be informed |
| 6 | **Mandatory field validation tested against a DB record with nulls** | Validates the server-side guard, not just request-body parsing |

def _generate_test_token(user: dict) -> str:
    """Generate a signed test JWT for the given user fixture."""
    import jwt as pyjwt  # PyJWT

    secret = "test-secret-key"
    payload = {
        "sub": user["id"],
        "username": user["usern
